page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8369B processors running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced resource allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on external storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with notable strengths in specific workloads. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA-NI. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark memory benchmarks demonstrate strong performance in the top 25% tier. Similarly, text generation for small language models achieves strong throughput at 304.16 tokens per second. Standard operations, such as Redis SET commands, static web serving, and Geekbench multi-core tasks, perform within the middle 50% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio make this instance a stable choice for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the overhead of specialized hardware. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing for flexible block storage attachments. This profile is highly suitable for enterprise applications, database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz, the instance provides 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute, database, and web serving tasks, with strong results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model text generation. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance, making it a cost-efficient option for enterprise applications, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose workloads.
